column10: The Raven server. RS64 processor at 125 MHz; 4-to-12-way SMP; PCI bus. Withdrawn December 13, 1999. Succeeded by S7A (RS64-II) and S80 (RS64-III).

lifecycle_current: Discontinued (RS64 line ended with POWER4 merger in 2001)
notes: IBM's first 64-bit PowerPC-AS commercial processor. Designed for transaction processing. RS64-II (262 MHz) and RS64-III (450 MHz) followed. POWER4 (2001) merged PowerPC and PowerPC-AS instruction sets.
Used in RS/6000 F50 (May 1997); also in Apple Power Mac G3 era Macs and Deep Blue chess computer.
IBM's Unix for RS/6000 and Power Systems. AIX 7.3 released 2022; active support through 2033. One of last surviving commercial Unix variants.

column10: Phase 1 of IBM's 1997 RS/6000 strategy; PowerPC 604e-based SMP announced April 1997. Aberdeen had forecast this.

notes: S70 delivered estimated 18,000 TPM on TPC benchmarks; TPC benchmarks remain standard for OLTP performance measurement.
